revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''佐波波地祇神社'''（さわわちぎじんじゃ/さわわくにつかみじんじゃ）は[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]にある神社。式内…」
住所が違っていたので訂正しました。
revision.text:
'''佐波波地祇神社'''（さわわちぎじんじゃ/さわわくにつかみじんじゃ）は[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]にある神社。[[式内小社]]。論社として以下の二社が存在する。
* [[佐波波地祇神社 (北茨城市大津町)

'''佐波波地祇神社'''（さわわちぎじんじゃ/さわわくにつかみじんじゃ）は[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]にある神社。[[常陸国]][[多賀郡|多珂郡]]の[[式内小社]]であり、同市内に論社とされる二社が存在する。 == 佐波波地祇神社（北茨城市大津町） == {{神社 |名称=佐波波地祇神社 |画像=[[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 49 | 緯度秒 = 59 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 32 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |所在地=[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]大津町1532 |祭神=[[天日方奇日方命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |例祭=[[5月3日]] |地図=Japan Ibaraki |ラベル位置=left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] === 概要 === [[北茨城市]]大津町の唐帰山（からかいさん）に鎮座する。社伝によると、[[日本武尊]]が武将を率いて東征した際、この沖で逆波に漂うこと数日、ある夜に神が雲に乗って枕頭に立ち「吾ハ佐波波神也、今皇子ノ船ヲ守護センガタメ来レリ、直チニ順風ト為サン」と告げ、夢から醒めると、果たしてその通りになったので、幣を奉り、佐波波の山に崇祀したという。その後は海上交通の守護神として尊崇され、逆風に会い異国に漂っても大神の助けがあって帰ってきた例があったため「唐帰の宮」と称した。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に[[郷社]]に列し、同4年（[[1871年]]）に[[村社]]となるが、同13年（[[1880年]]）9月に再び郷社に復した。 === 祭神 === [[天日方奇日方命]]を主祭神とし、[[大国主|大己貴命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]]、[[大物主|大物主命]]、姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命、五十鈴依姫命を配祀する。 === 境内 === 本殿は流造正面軒唐破風銅葺で、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の建立。拝殿は[[明治]]11年（[[1878年]]）に造営された旧拝殿があったが、[[平成]]30年（[[2018年]]）に解体され、新拝殿が平成31年（[[2019年]]）に竣工した。 File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|末社（稲荷神社） === 御船祭 === 当社の春季例祭は、5年に一回'''御船祭'''と呼ばれる大祭が5月2日から3日にかけて催行される。平成29年（[[2017年]]）3月には「常陸大津の御船祭」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 *[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]大津町1532 ; 交通アクセス *[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[常磐線]][[大津港駅]]から東へ徒歩30分。 === 外部リンク === *[http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/link/0013.html 佐波波地祇神社]‐茨城県神社庁 == 佐波々地祇神社（北茨城市華川町上小津田） == {{神社 |名称=佐波々地祇神社 |画像= |緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 49 | 緯度秒 = 39 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 42 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |所在地=[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 |祭神=[[天日方奇日方命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |例祭=[[11月23日]] |地図=Japan Ibaraki |ラベル位置=left }} === 概要 === [[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田にある塩原山の中腹に鎮座している。[[日本武尊]]が東征の帰途にこの地を過ぎて佐波神を拝し、祀ったことを創祀とする。元は現社地の北西約4キロにある佐波山（上小津田字二田）に鎮座していたとされ、[[永禄]]12年([[1569年]])に塩原山の山上に遷座し、その後山腹の現社地に遷座したという。[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月[[郷社]]に列した。 === 祭神 === [[天日方奇日方命]]を祀る。 === 現地情報 === *[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さわわちきしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]]

旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |例祭=[[11月23日]] |地図=Japan Ibaraki |ラベル位置=left }} === 概要 === [[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田にある塩原山の中腹に鎮座している。[[日本武尊]]が東征の帰途にこの地を過ぎて佐波神を拝し、祀ったことを創祀とする。元は現社地の北西約4キロにある佐波山（上小津田字二田）に鎮座していたとされ、[[永禄]]12年（[[1569年]]）に塩原山の山上に遷座し、その後山腹の現社地に遷座したという。[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月[[郷社]]に列した。 === 祭神 === [[天日方奇日方命]]を祀る。 === 現地情報 === *[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さわわちきしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]]

revision.text:
'''佐波波地祇神社'''（さわわちぎじんじゃ/さわわくにつかみじんじゃ）は[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]にある神社。[[常陸国]][[多賀郡|多珂郡]]の[[式内小社]]であり、同市内に論社とされる二社が存在する。 == 佐波波地祇神社（北茨城市大津町） == {{神社 |名称=佐波波地祇神社 |画像=[[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 49 | 緯度秒 = 59 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 47 | 経度秒 = 32 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |所在地=[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]大津町1532 |祭神=[[天日方奇日方命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |例祭=[[5月3日]] |神事=御船祭（5年に1度、[[5月2日]]～3日）
潮和祭（[[11月3日]]） |地図=Japan Ibaraki |ラベル位置=left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] === 概要 === [[北茨城市]]大津町の唐帰山（からかいさん）に鎮座する。社伝によると、[[日本武尊]]が武将を率いて東征した際、この沖で逆波に漂うこと数日、ある夜に神が雲に乗って枕頭に立ち「吾ハ佐波波神也、今皇子ノ船ヲ守護センガタメ来レリ、直チニ順風ト為サン」と告げ、夢から醒めると、果たしてその通りになったので、幣を奉り、佐波波の山に崇祀したという。その後は海上交通の守護神として尊崇され、逆風に会い異国に漂っても大神の助けがあって帰ってきた例があったため「唐帰の宮」と称した。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に[[郷社]]に列し、同4年（[[1871年]]）に[[村社]]となるが、同13年（[[1880年]]）9月に再び郷社に復した。 === 祭神 === [[天日方奇日方命]]を主祭神とし、[[大国主|大己貴命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]]、[[大物主|大物主命]]、姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命、五十鈴依姫命を配祀する。 === 境内 === 本殿は流造正面軒唐破風銅葺で、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の建立。拝殿は[[明治]]11年（[[1878年]]）に造営された旧拝殿があったが、[[平成]]30年（[[2018年]]）に解体され、新拝殿が平成31年（[[2019年]]）に竣工した。 File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|末社（稲荷神社） === 御船祭 === 当社の春季例祭は、5年に1度'''御船祭'''と呼ばれる大祭が5月2日から3日にかけて催行される。平成29年（[[2017年]]）3月には「常陸大津の御船祭」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 *[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]大津町1532 ; 交通アクセス *[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[常磐線]][[大津港駅]]から東へ徒歩30分。 === 外部リンク === *[http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/link/0013.html 佐波波地祇神社]‐茨城県神社庁 == 佐波々地祇神社（北茨城市華川町上小津田） == {{神社 |名称=佐波々地祇神社 |画像= |緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 49 | 緯度秒 = 39 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 42 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |所在地=[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 |祭神=[[天日方奇日方命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名=佐波神社 |例祭=[[11月23日]] |地図=Japan Ibaraki |ラベル位置=left }} === 概要 === [[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田にある塩原山の中腹に鎮座している。社伝によると、[[日本武尊]]が東征の帰途にこの地を過ぎて佐波神を拝し、祀ったことを創祀とする。元は現社地の北西約4キロにある佐波山（上小津田字二田）に鎮座していたとされ、[[永禄]]12年（[[1569年]]）に塩原山の山上に遷座し、その後山腹の現社地に遷座したという。[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月[[郷社]]に列した。 === 祭神 === [[天日方奇日方命]]を祀る。また祭神の別名を[[阿多都久志尼命]]とも[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|多気佐波夜遅奴美命]]とも言い、「佐波」の名前はここから来るとも。 === 現地情報 === *[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さわわちきしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]]

拝殿 |緯度度 = 36 | 緯度分 = 49 | 緯度秒 = 39 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 140 |経度分 = 42 | 経度秒 = 42 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |所在地=[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 |祭神=[[天日方奇日方命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社
旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名=佐波神社 |例祭=[[11月23日]] |地図=Japan Ibaraki |ラベル位置=left }} === 概要 === [[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田にある塩原山の中腹に鎮座している。社伝によると、[[日本武尊]]が東征の帰途にこの地を過ぎて佐波神を拝し、祀ったことを創祀とする。元は現社地の北西約4キロにある佐波山（上小津田字二田）に鎮座していたとされ、[[永禄]]12年（[[1569年]]）に塩原山の山上に遷座し、その後山腹の現社地に遷座したという。[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月[[郷社]]に列した。 === 祭神 === [[天日方奇日方命]]を祀る。また祭神の別名を[[阿多都久志尼命]]とも[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|多気佐波夜遅奴美命]]とも言い、「佐波」の名前はここから来るとも。 === 境内 === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|末社 === 現地情報 === *[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さわわちきしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]]

旧[[郷社]] |創建=不詳 |本殿=[[流造]]銅板葺 |別名=佐波神社 |例祭=[[11月23日]] |地図={{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=佐波々地祇神社社|position=center|width=200|float=center}} |ラベル位置=left }} === 概要 === [[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田にある塩原山の中腹に鎮座している。社伝によると、[[日本武尊]]が東征の帰途にこの地を過ぎて佐波神を拝し、祀ったことを創祀とする。元は現社地の北西約4キロにある佐波山（上小津田字二田）に鎮座していたとされ、[[永禄]]12年（[[1569年]]）に塩原山の山上に遷座し、その後山腹の現社地に遷座したという。[[明治]]7年（[[1874年]]）5月[[郷社]]に列した。 === 祭神 === [[天日方奇日方命]]を祀る。また祭神の別名を[[阿多都久志尼命]]とも[[速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神|多気佐波夜遅奴美命]]とも言い、「佐波」の名前はここから来るとも。 === 境内 === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|末社 === 現地情報 === *[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さわわちきしんしや}} [[Category:茨城県の神社]] [[Category:常陸国の式内社]] [[Category:茨城県の旧郷社]]

潮和祭（[[11月3日]]） |地図=Japan Ibaraki |ラベル位置=left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|鳥居}}]] === 概要 === [[北茨城市]]大津町の唐帰山（からかいさん）に鎮座する。社伝によると、[[日本武尊]]が武将を率いて東征した際、この沖で逆波に漂うこと数日、ある夜に神が雲に乗って枕頭に立ち「吾ハ佐波波神也、今皇子ノ船ヲ守護センガタメ来レリ、直チニ順風ト為サン」と告げ、夢から醒めると、果たしてその通りになったので、幣を奉り、佐波波の山に崇祀したという。その後は海上交通の守護神として尊崇され、逆風に会い異国に漂っても大神の助けがあって帰ってきた例があったため「唐帰の宮」と称した。[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）に[[郷社]]に列し、同4年（[[1871年]]）に[[村社]]となるが、同13年（[[1880年]]）9月に再び郷社に復した。 === 祭神 === [[天日方奇日方命]]を主祭神とし、[[大国主|大己貴命]]、[[事代主|事代主命]]、[[大物主|大物主命]]、姫蹈鞴五十鈴姫命、五十鈴依姫命を配祀する。 === 境内 === 本殿は流造正面軒唐破風銅葺で、[[享保]]12年（[[1727年]]）の建立。拝殿は[[明治]]11年（[[1878年]]）に造営された旧拝殿があったが、[[平成]]30年（[[2018年]]）に解体され、新拝殿が平成31年（[[2019年]]）に竣工した。 File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|本殿 File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|末社（稲荷神社） === 御船祭 === 当社の春季例祭は、5年に1度'''御船祭'''と呼ばれる大祭が5月2日から3日にかけて催行される。平成29年（[[2017年]]）3月には「[[常陸大津の御船祭]]」として国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 *[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]大津町1532 ; 交通アクセス *[[東日本旅客鉄道]]（JR東日本）[[常磐線]][[大津港駅]]から東へ徒歩30分。 === 外部リンク === *[http://www.oyashiro.or.jp/link/0013.html 佐波波地祇神社]‐茨城県神社庁 == 佐波々地祇神社（北茨城市華川町上小津田） == {{神社 |名称=佐波々地祇神社 |画像=[[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 |位置={{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |所在地=[[茨城県]][[北茨城市]]華川町上小津田1233 |祭神=[[天日方奇日方命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）論社

revision.text:
[[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |Image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |Location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |Deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Annual festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * Location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |Image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |Location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |Deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |Annual festival = November 23 |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === Deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * Location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]]

[[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |Image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |Location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |Deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Annual festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * Location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |Image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |Location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |Deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |Annual festival = November 23 |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === Deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * Location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:new pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]]

Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * Location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |Image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |Location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |Deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |Annual festival = November 23 |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === Deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * Location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]]

One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * Location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |Image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |Location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |Deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |Annual festival = November 23 |Map = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === Deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * Location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:ファイル]]

The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]][[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:dd]]

The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]]

[[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |Name = Sawawachigi Shrine |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |Founded = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]]

Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]]

[[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Shikinaisha of Hitachi Province]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Former Gōsha]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:bump]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]]

Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] revision.text:
[[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Sawawachigi [[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Shrine |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Sawawachigi revision.text:
[[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] {{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}} === Overview === This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命}} |Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} === Overview === This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Sawawachigi Shrine]][[Category:Sawawachigi
