Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] == Overview == This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|qq=}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Port Station|qq=}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]

Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} == Overview == This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|佐波波地祇神社|version=91348590|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Sawawachigi-jinja (Hanakawa, Kitaibaraki)}} {{wikidata link|Q56352076}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Sawawachigi-jinja (Hanakawa, Kitaibaraki)]] [[da:d:Q56352076]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Sawawachigi Shrine]] [[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] [[Category:Architecture of Kitaibaraki City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]

revision.text:
{{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaibaraki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Province|qq=}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] == Overview == This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|qq=}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Port Station|qq=}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]

According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|佐波波地祇神社|version=91348590|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Sawawachigi Shrine (Q56352076)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|WD=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] == Overview == This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsukō Station|en|Ōtsukō Station|WD=Q5353392|lt=Ōtsu Port Station|1=Ōtsukō Station}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]

Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] == Overview == This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsukō Station|en|Ōtsukō Station|WD=Q5353392|lt=Ōtsu Port Station|1=Ōtsukō Station}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]

According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|May 3|WD=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] == Overview == This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsukō Station|en|Ōtsukō Station|WD=Q5353392|lt=Ōtsu Port Station|1=Ōtsukō Station}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]

{{Ref improve|date=August 2020}} '''Sawawachigi Shrine''' (佐波波地祇神社, Sawawachigi Jinja/Sawawakunitsukami Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kitaibaraki City|ja|北茨城市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitaibaraki City|qq=}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ibaraki Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor shrine) of the {{ill|Hitachi Province|ja|常陸国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Province|qq=}}, with two shrines in the city considered its possible locations. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Haiden2.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |Latitude degrees = 36 |Latitude minutes = 49 |Latitude seconds = 59 |N = N |Longitude degrees = 140 |Longitude minutes = 47 |Longitude seconds = 32 |E = E |location = Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagare-zukuri|qq=}}, copper roof |festival = {{ill|May 3|ja|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} |Rituals = Ofune Festival (once every five years, May 2–3)
Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] == Overview == This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|qq=}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|qq=}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|qq=}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsu Port Station|ja|大津港駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtsu Port Station|qq=}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|qq=}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (minor shrine), disputed site

According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|佐波波地祇神社|version=91348590|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Sawawachigi Shrine (Q56352076)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. Shioai Festival (November 3) |Map = Japan Ibaraki |Label position = left }} [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{Center|Torii}}]] == Overview == This shrine is located on Mount Karakai in Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to shrine tradition, during {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}’s eastern campaign, his fleet was caught in adverse waves for several days. One night, the deity appeared in his dream, saying, "I am the deity of Sawawa. I have come to protect your ships. I will calm the winds immediately." Upon awakening, the winds had indeed calmed. Yamato Takeru dedicated offerings and enshrined the deity on Mount Sawawa. The shrine became revered as a protector of maritime traffic, earning the name "Karagai no Miya" ("Shrine of Returning from Tang"). In 1869, it was Ranked as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} but was later demoted to {{ill|Sonsha|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sonsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} in 1871. In 1880, it was reinstated as a gōsha. === Deities === The main deity is {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}}. Other deities enshrined include: - {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} - {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} - {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主命|1=Ōmononushi|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} - Hime Tatara Isuzu-hime no Mikoto - Isuzuyori-hime no Mikoto === Grounds === The honden, built in 1727 during the Kyōhō era, is of the nagare-zukuri style with a copper roof. The former haiden, constructed in 1878, was dismantled in 2018, and a new haiden was completed in 2019. File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Otsucho Massha.jpg|Massha (Inari Shrine) === Ofune Festival === The spring festival is held once every five years, known as the "Ofune Festival," spanning May 2–3. In 2017, it was designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}} by the Japanese government under the name "Hitachi Ōtsu no Ofune Matsuri." === Access === * location: Ōtsu-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture * From {{ill|Ōtsukō Station|en|Ōtsukō Station|WD=Q5353392|lt=Ōtsu Port Station|1=Ōtsukō Station}} (JR Jōban Line), walk 30 minutes east. == Sawawachigi Shrine (Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City) == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Sawawachigi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Haiden |coordinates = {{coord|36|49|39|N|140|42|42|E|display=inline}} |location = Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture |deity = {{ill|Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja|天日方奇日方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鴨王 (上古)|1=Kamo no Okimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55809532}} |Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (minor shrine), disputed site
Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Honden style = {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}}, copper roof |Alternate name = Sawa Shrine |festival = November 23 |Map = {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=49|lat_sec=39|lon_deg=140|lon_min=42|lon_sec=42|label=Sawawachigi Shrine|position=center|width=200}} |Label position = left }} == Overview == This shrine is located on the slopes of Mount Shiobara in Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City. According to tradition, Yamato Takeru worshipped the Sawa deity here on his return journey from his eastern campaign. Initially located on Mount Sawa, about 4 kilometers northwest of the current site, the shrine was moved to the mountain summit in 1569 and later relocated to its present position on the slope. === deity === The enshrined deity is Amahikata Kihikata no Mikoto, also known as: - Atatsukushinimikoto (阿多都久志尼命) - Takemisakasawayanosonuminomikoto (速甕之多気佐波夜遅奴美神) === Grounds === File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Honden.jpg|Honden File:Sawawachigi Jinja Hanakawacho Massha.jpg|Massha === Access === * location: Hanakawa-chō, Kitaibaraki City, Ibaraki Prefecture {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Ibaraki Prefecture Shrines Index}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|佐波波地祇神社|version=91348590|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Sawawachigi-jinja (Hanakawa, Kitaibaraki)}} {{wikidata link|Q56352076}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Sawawachigi-jinja (Hanakawa, Kitaibaraki)]] [[da:d:Q56352076]] [[Sawawachigi Shrine]] [[ja:佐波波地祇神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Municipal History of Kitaibaraki]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Sixth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]]
